title: Infinity Mod Forge — Internal Mod Data
description: Schema for Forge-internal per-mod JSON files under `data/mods/*.json`. Distinct from the author-facing `mod-forge.schema.json` (which authors include in their own repositories with verbose field names) — this schema governs the compact-named curated data served to the Forge UI and the runner. Not all fields are required; the goal is to validate the fields we DO use. For the evidence-grading model on conflicts/advisories/ki entries, see docs/EVIDENCE_GRADING.md.

i.description: Numeric Forge mod ID, stable across releases.

au.description: Primary author handle (used for same-author conflict detection).

ord.description: Relative install-order rank within the mod's category.

cats.description: All categories this mod appears under.

with.description: TP2 folder name of the other mod (lowercase).

withId.description: Forge numeric ID of the other mod — lets the UI resolve the target without a name lookup.

severity.description: hard = mutually exclusive, partial = specific components conflict, soft = possible issues.

evidenceLevel.description: How strongly this conflict is supported by evidence. `observed` = an actual install produced the failure (session ID / log excerpt must be cited). `mechanism-verified` = code paths in both mods have been traced and the failure mode is mechanically plausible, but not yet seen in practice. `speculative` = plausible but unverified — belongs in `advisories[]`, not `conflicts[]`. Three tiers get different UI styling. Added 2026-04 after the Stormlord↔EPS audit revealed 1163 entries with only 8 carrying actual session evidence.

sessionId.description: Required when `evidenceLevel = "observed"`. 8+ hex char session ID from `gui.log` or the `Test #N` tag. Lets reviewers pull the preserved WSETUP.DEBUG to verify the claim.

source.description: Provenance note — upstream README/advisory, forum thread URL, code-trace rationale, curator note. Distinct from `sessionId`.

orderOnly.description: True when recommended install order resolves the conflict. UI can demote order-only entries from the top-severity view.

reason.description: Why these mods conflict. Prefer concrete mechanism (`Mod A calls COPY_EXISTING X; Mod B’s cleanup removes X from override before A runs`) over vague phrasing (`may interact with`). If reason uses `may`/`might`/`could`/`likely`/`probably`, `evidenceLevel` should be `speculative`.

myComps.description: Which of YOUR component numbers are involved (e.g. "#0 #10" or "#15 Pre-Buffing").

theirComps.description: Which of THEIR component numbers are involved.

pattern.description: Regex pattern matched against preserved WSETUP.DEBUG / install.log by the runner. Used for both diagnostic display (DebugPanel) and future evidence upgrades (when a `speculative` conflict's pattern fires in the wild, it can be promoted to `observed`).

category.description: Classifier chip shown in the UI (e.g. `cosmetic`, `likely-benign`, `caution`, `concerning`, `unknown`).

evidenceLevel.description: Same semantics as on `conflict`. A `speculative` ki pattern is still useful (runner still scans for it) but the UI should mark it as "unverified detector" until at least one session matches it.
